title: 2020 BCI competition, track 3
description: The 2020 BCI Competition Track 3 dataset comprises EEG recordings from 15 subjects performing imagined speech tasks involving five distinct commands: 'Hello', 'Help me', 'Stop', 'Thank you', and 'Yes'. Designed for brain-computer interface research, the dataset includes 64-channel EEG recordings sampled at 256 Hz, organized into training, validation, and test sets with annotated trial onsets and durations. The data has been converted to BIDS-compliant EDF format with preserved channel montage information and temporal event markers for each imagined speech trial.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded from participants performing imagined speech tasks where subjects mentally articulated five different phrases without audible speech or overt mouth movements. Data were acquired using a 64-channel EEG system at 256 Hz sampling rate. The original MATLAB and HDF5 files were converted to BIDS-compliant EDF format, with EEG data scaled to Volts (multiplied by 1e-6). Epoched data were concatenated into continuous recordings with event annotations marking trial onsets and durations for each imagined speech command.
